skill.name: systematic-review-screener
skill.description: Automated abstract screening tool for systematic literature reviews with PRISMA workflow support.
skill.summary: Automated abstract screening tool for systematic literature reviews with PRISMA workflow support.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 97 static analysis findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown code block backticks as shell execution (66 findings), YAML keys and descriptive text as weak cryptography (9 findings), and security checklist references to path traversal as actual traversal (2 findings). The script reads reference files, processes abstracts against inclusion/exclusion criteria, and writes output files to a specified directory. No malicious intent, credential access, network exfiltration, or dangerous code patterns were identified. This is a legitimate literature review automation tool.

content.user_title: Screen Academic Abstracts for Systematic Reviews
content.value_statement: Manually screening hundreds of abstracts for a systematic review is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates the screening process against custom criteria and generates PRISMA-compliant outputs with confidence scores.
actual_capabilities: Screen academic abstracts against custom inclusion and exclusion criteria from a YAML configuration file
Parse references from multiple formats including CSV, TSV, PubMed MEDLINE, and EndNote XML
Generate PRISMA 2020 flow diagram data for systematic review reporting
Flag low-confidence decisions for manual human review with detailed decision rationale
Export screening results as CSV or JSON with full decision trail and matched criteria
Process large reference sets in configurable batch sizes with progress reporting
limitations: Provides screening recommendations only; final inclusion decisions require human verification
Optimized for English-language abstracts; language detection for other languages is heuristic-based
Performs best on structured abstracts with clear Background, Methods, Results, and Conclusion sections
Criteria must be manually configured for each research question; no auto-generated criteria

use_cases.title: Medical Researcher Conducting a Meta-Analysis
use_cases.description: A researcher screens over 2000 abstracts from PubMed against PICO criteria to identify studies for a meta-analysis on diabetes treatments.

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Preparing a Thesis Literature Review
use_cases.description: A PhD student screens search results from multiple databases for their dissertation literature review, tracking decisions with PRISMA workflow.

use_cases.title: Evidence-Based Practice Team in Healthcare
use_cases.description: A hospital research team screens clinical trial abstracts against treatment protocols to inform clinical practice guidelines.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Abstract Screening
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has a CSV file of references and a criteria template.
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a CSV file of references from my database search. Please help me screen them using the systematic-review-screener skill. Set up the criteria file based on my research question and run the screening.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Criteria Configuration
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs to define specific inclusion and exclusion criteria.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a custom criteria.yaml file for my systematic review on [topic]. I want to include [study types], focus on [population], and require [intervention]. Exclude [exclusion criteria]. Then run the screening on my input file.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Format PRISMA Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs full PRISMA-compliant screening with multiple output formats.
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to conduct a full systematic review screening on references in PubMed format. Load the criteria from criteria.yaml, run with a confidence threshold of 0.8, generate PRISMA flow data, and export results as JSON to the output directory.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Processing with Conflict Export
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user processing a large reference set with conflict-only export.
prompt_templates.prompt: I have 5000 references to screen from an XML export. Configure batch size of 250, set confidence threshold to 0.7, generate PRISMA data, and export only conflicts to a separate file for manual review. Show the screening summary statistics.

output_examples.input: CSV file with 50 medical abstracts from a PubMed search
output_examples.output: Screening summary: 12 included, 30 excluded, 8 flagged for review. Output files: screened_included.csv, screened_excluded.csv, conflicts.csv, and screening_log.json with full decision trail.

output_examples.input: PubMed .txt export with 200 cardiovascular research abstracts and custom criteria.yaml
output_examples.output: Full screening log with rationale for each reference. 45 records passed, 120 excluded, 35 require manual review. PRISMA flow diagram data generated for inclusion in systematic review report.

output_examples.input: EndNote XML file with 100 mixed-format references and strict inclusion criteria
output_examples.output: Batch screening results: 28 included with confidence > 0.8, 52 excluded with specific reasons, 20 conflicts requiring human verification. Conflict-only CSV exported for second reviewer.

best_practices: Review the criteria.yaml configuration before running screening to ensure inclusion and exclusion rules match your research question.
Manually review all flagged conflict records, as they represent edge cases where the automated screening has low confidence.
Validate screening decisions on a small test set of 10 to 20 abstracts before processing large reference batches.
anti_patterns: Relying solely on automated screening results without human verification for final inclusion decisions in systematic reviews.
Using the default criteria template without customizing study type, population, and intervention parameters for your research question.
Processing abstracts in languages other than English without manually verifying language detection results.

faq.question: What input formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports CSV, TSV, PubMed MEDLINE format (.txt), and EndNote XML files. Required columns for CSV are title and abstract.

faq.question: Can this skill replace human reviewers for systematic reviews?
faq.answer: No. This is a screening assistant that provides recommendations. Final inclusion decisions must always be verified by human reviewers.

faq.question: How does confidence scoring work?
faq.answer: Confidence scores range from 0 to 1.0 based on keyword match quality. Records below the configured threshold are flagged for manual review.

faq.question: What is PRISMA support?
faq.answer: The skill generates structured data for PRISMA 2020 flow diagrams, including counts for identification, screening, and inclusion stages.

faq.question: How do I define my own screening criteria?
faq.answer: Edit the references/criteria_template.yaml file to set study types, population keywords, intervention requirements, language, and year range for your review.

faq.question: Can I process very large reference sets?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports batch processing with configurable batch sizes, defaulting to 100 records per batch for efficient handling of large sets.
